transcript_text:
spk_0: There it goes... Okay, Adam, so let's start by getting you to tell us a bit about yourself.
spk_0: What do you currently do?
spk_0: Are you studying currently?
spk_0: Are you a past student?
spk_0: Where do you live? Things like that.
spk_1: So, I live in a small town in County Cavan, in Ireland.
spk_1: I'm currently studying for a Bachelor of Arts degree at Maynooth University.
spk_1: I'm currently in my second year, so I still have...
spk_1: I'm doing four years of my course, so I have two more years left.
spk_1: So, I usually commute to school because accommodation in Dublin is extremely expensive.
spk_1: I'm trying to think...
spk_1: it's just like not all coming to my head now.
spk_1: Yeah, that's all I can think of, really.
spk_2: Yeah... that's grand.
spk_0: So, second year... two years left.
spk_0: What, then, does a typical week look like for you if you're having to commute as well?
spk_1: So, usually waking up at around 5 o'clock, and I live in Ballyconnell, so it's kind of a small town, and it's about 20, 25 minutes from Cavan town.
spk_1: So, I take the 109X bus down to Dublin, and then I have to take the Dublin bus down to Maynooth, or I usually go by train up in Connolly Station, as I usually use the train.
spk_1: So, kind of waking up at 5 o'clock, getting on the bus at 6, and then trying to be there around 9 or 10 o'clock...
spk_1: Kind of a long commute!
spk_0: Wow, that is huge! So, what I'm trying to do...
spk_0: like, quick math here...
spk_0: to figure out, what was that, like, four hours?
spk_1: Around four... Yeah, around four to five hours.
spk_1: It's crazy! But I, I do stay in Dublin with a friend.
spk_1: Like, I do pay him on the regular, so if I'm...
spk_1: say my lectures are full week, Monday to Friday, then I just stay with him.
spk_1: I ask him if he's...
spk_1: like, I'm sleeping on the couch and stuff.
spk_1: Like, I don't want to be traveling four hours a day and all that.
spk_0: Wow, okay, that's insane!
spk_0: Okay, so you have, like, a part-time residence in Dublin, which is so interesting...
spk_0: How much of your week, then, is, like, contact hours versus independent study?
spk_1: So, usually, it's...
spk_1: lectures vary, so it's usually...
spk_1: I don't want to lie.
spk_1: So, it's around four hours to five hours in a day.
spk_1: But sometimes it might be shorter, might be even a half day...
spk_1: so might be three hours.
spk_1: It really depends. So, because our timetable is kind of like varying because some...
spk_1: some of our lecturers keep leaving, and we keep getting new ones for some reason, and yeah, it just keeps wearing and varying.
spk_1: I think it should be back to normal in around March.
spk_2: Wow.
spk_0: So, you don't have any, like, three days...?
spk_0: It's... everything's...?
spk_0: You're in there every day?
spk_1: Pretty much for now, yeah...
spk_1: but I do have the weekend: Saturday and Sunday off, of course.
spk_2: Wow! Okay, that's crazy.
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: What about extracurricular activities, then?
spk_1: Yeah, I do myself...
spk_1: Gaa. I play Gaelic; I like to play basketball; I like to do some football on the side, and yeah, just socialize with people.
spk_2: Great.
spk_0: And then, are you living at home with...
spk_0: with family?
spk_1: Yeah... with family, yeah.
spk_2: Great.
spk_0: What about social media, then?
spk_0: What social media platforms are you currently using?
spk_1: So, Instagram, WhatsApp, TikTok, iMessage...
spk_1: Yeah, that's all I can think of, really.
spk_0: Which would you use the most for communicating?
spk_1: I'd use WhatsApp, and then second would be Instagram.
spk_0: Okay... And roughly, how much time a day would you reckon you spend on social media?
spk_1: A lot... definitely on the weekends, I'm spending at least six to seven hours on it.
spk_1: But on weekdays, when I'm busy, I wouldn't be allowed three to four hours, maybe even less.
spk_2: Okay, perfect.

spk_0: Online?
spk_1: I see a lot on Facebook...
spk_1: I used... I see a lot on TikTok and YouTube as well.
spk_1: Like, I keep seeing the promotions: Just Eat, Deliveroo, UberEats.
spk_1: I have a subscription for Uber One.
spk_1: I have a subscription for Deliveroo Plus.
spk_1: I don't have anything for Just Eat...
spk_1: That's it, only those two.

spk_1: So, I have Prime. So, I just got on top of Prime.
spk_1: But out of all the apps, I'd be using Uber.
spk_1: So, that's the app that I pay the monthly subscription for.

spk_0: Okay, so it's like two...
spk_0: Well, this is what I'm going off—UK numbers—but it's like 249 in...

spk_1: yeah. Five euro... yeah, yeah, it's definitely.
spk_0: Yeah, five years...
spk_0: Yeah, yeah. Okay, great!

spk_1: Okay, well, yeah, 10.99.

spk_1: I do follow food influencers, restaurants as well, and all of that.
spk_2: Perfect... that's great!

spk_1: this kebab place. So I went on delivery, and I just ordered it.
spk_1: Yeah, I do... if I see something on...
spk_1: on Instagram.

spk_0: and you ordered via delivery?
spk_1: Yeah.

spk_1: so that was the main reason why I got it.

spk_0: morning to night. What do you eat, when, and where?

spk_1: so that's on Mondays.
spk_1: Usually a quick breakfast.
spk_1: So it'd be cereal? Yeah, usually cereal, Weetabix, or anything like that...
spk_1: toast. Just the small stuff.
spk_1: And then around 12 o'clock, I'd be having my lunch, so that'd be my...
spk_1: like my main stuff.
spk_1: Usually, when I'm not at home, I'm always ordering off Just Eat, Deliveroo, or UberEats, and I'm ordering to the friend's house.
spk_1: So about pretty much five times a week, maybe even more if I'm ordering dinner as well.
spk_1: Ordering off those apps?
spk_1: Yeah!

spk_0: to actually go and visit the places physically?
spk_1: Yeah, definitely.

spk_1: I'm looking at price...
spk_1: Yeah, pretty much price, promotion, quality, and reviews.

spk_1: The stars... because you can't see, like, the people's comments.
spk_1: So if I see something that's like three stars, that's gonna, like, throw me off.
spk_1: But if I see something like 4 stars or 4.5 stars, then yeah, that's getting it in!
spk_0: That's great! So, obviously, you're doing a lot of commuting...
spk_0: a lot of traveling for uni.
spk_0: So, how often are you cooking?
spk_1: Oh, maybe twice a week...
spk_1: Definitely twice a week.
spk_1: I wouldn't say more.
spk_1: Okay, twice a week.
spk_2: Yeah.

spk_1: So, I do enjoy cooking, but I don't have the time to do it.
spk_1: So, I like to throw in the pan myself...
spk_1: cook... cook a nice dinner, like spaghetti bolognese or a curry.
spk_1: Like, I like cooking myself, but I just don't have the time to do it.
spk_2: Absolutely. Okay.
spk_0: Yeah... imagine with that much traveling!
spk_0: So then, how often do you order food and groceries for delivery?
spk_1: So, I'm trying to think...
spk_1: more than five times a week.
spk_1: I'll tell you that for sure!
spk_2: Okay.

spk_1: Always in Dublin, and sometimes up in Cavan as well, because I just eat started releasing their own, like couriers in Cavan.
spk_1: So I did test it out once, about two times...
spk_1: Yeah. And yeah, it was like the same in Dublin.
spk_1: The fellas coming on the bikes or in the car, and they just give you the food.

spk_1: Delivery, mostly.

spk_1: I noticed, like, my favorite Chinese is on there, my favorite Indian's on there.
spk_1: But, like, if I'm ordering, like, McDonald's or something like that, I'd go into UberEats because UberEats usually offers a better discount or promotion on it.

spk_0: delivery.
spk_1: Yeah... delivery...
spk_1: yeah.

spk_0: For... for me, I find lunch is quite stressful because I don't really have a lot of time to have.
spk_1: Maybe lunch and dinner would be stressful.
spk_1: I'll say lunch would be, because you're running through classes, and you're trying to feed yourself...
spk_1: definitely, lunch would be the most stressful.

spk_1: the food.

spk_1: So, comforting is like when I'm inside...
spk_1: I like, I have a day off, mostly that.
spk_1: And then food socials, like when I'm going out and having a great time with my friends, mostly.

spk_0: Like I said, there's no right or wrong answers here.
spk_0: It's really up to you.

spk_1: healthier. But yeah, mostly those stuff.
spk_1: Yeah, or like a roll or a wrap...
spk_1: something like that.

spk_2: Okay.

spk_0: Obviously, you're not doing a lot of cooking...

spk_1: Yeah.

spk_1: No, Deliveroo or Uber.

spk_2: Fantastic.
spk_0: Okay, so, would you say it was a planned order, or was it pretty spontaneous?

spk_1: From the morning, I was already planning what to get.
spk_1: And then in the evening...
spk_1: then... yeah.

spk_0: yeah, most.

spk_1: yeah.

spk_1: I'll just continue ordering with the Chinese and all that.

spk_1: Yeah.

spk_1: if it's like Friday, or I don't have lecture on, then I'm like, ah, screw it, I'll just go down to the place and just get the food.

spk_1: and yeah, discounts would be number one on it, and most...
spk_1: yeah, mostly discounts.

spk_1: or like, get free food on top of that.

spk_0: Is there anything that you would say makes getting delivery feel indulgent...

spk_1: I used to tip the delivery drivers, and I'm kind of feeling bad because I know they can see on their screens that you're not, like, putting in a tip or, like, something like that.
spk_1: So I'm kind of feeling guilty, and as well, most of the time, like, I feel bad for myself because I'm, like, staying at home, not going out most of the time because I don't have the time.
spk_1: And I'm like, I know I have the time to do this, get the food, but instead, I'm just ordering from the place.
